issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module’s open() and close() functions change the visual state of the hamburger button but never set aria‑expanded on the trigger element. Consequently screen‑reader users cannot know whether the navigation drawer is open. In addition, the trigger’s aria‑expanded state is not initialised on page load.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu module’s _openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions show/hide panels but never update the aria‑expanded attribute on the trigger button. The attribute is also not set to false on page load, leaving assistive technology unaware of the menu’s collapsed state.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open action does not set aria‑expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: In faqAccordion.open() the panel is revealed, but the corresponding trigger button’s aria‑expanded attribute is never set to true. Only the close path resets it to false. This leaves the state ambiguous for assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period toggle buttons do not update aria‑pressed (state)
issuesFound.explanation: The set of period buttons (`data-period`) are used as a radio‑style toggle, but the code only adds/removes a CSS class. No aria‑pressed or aria‑selected attribute is updated, so screen‑reader users cannot perceive which period is active.

issuesFound.title: Simple billing‑toggle switch does not update aria‑pressed (state)
issuesFound.explanation: The on/off billing toggle (`data-billing-toggle`) is given role="switch” but never receives an aria‑pressed value. Users of assistive technology cannot tell whether the switch is on (annual) or off (monthly).

issuesFound.title: Form validation does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator module validates fields and displays error messages, but it never adds aria‑invalid="true" to the offending input nor removes it on success. Assistive technologies therefore cannot detect validation errors programmatically.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic pricing changes are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period changes, price numbers on all plan cards are updated, but no ARIA live region is used to announce the change. Users of screen readers may miss the updated pricing information.

issuesFound.title: aria‑expanded not initialised on page load for dropdown menus (MEDIUM)
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu init function registers triggers but never sets aria‑expanded="false" on them, leaving the attribute undefined until the user interacts. This violates the requirement that toggle widgets expose their collapsed state on load.

issuesFound.title: aria‑expanded not initialised on page load for mobile navigation (MEDIUM)
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button is given aria‑expanded only when toggled; its initial state is undefined, which confuses assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix:
(see Issue 1 fix – the same setAttr call in init adds the missing initialisation.)

issuesFound.explanation: AT cannot know whether the suggestion list is currently visible.

issuesFound.explanation: Users of assistive technology cannot tell which billing period is active.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `.nav-toggle` button that opens and closes the mobile navigation drawer never receives an `aria‑expanded` attribute. Screen‑reader users cannot tell whether the menu is currently expanded or collapsed, violating WCAG 2.1 ARIA practices for disclosure widgets.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', String(_open));` inside the `open()` and `close()` functions (or within `toggle()`) so the attribute reflects the drawer state.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger buttons do not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons that open dropdown panels (identified by `[data-has-dropdown]` or `[data-dropdown]`) are given `aria‑haspopup` but never receive an `aria‑expanded` state change when the panel opens or closes. This leaves assistive technology unaware of the panel’s visibility.
issuesFound.fix: In `_openInstance()` add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` and in `_closeInstance()` add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Breadcrumb navigation missing aria‑current on the last item
issuesFound.explanation: The `_processNav` function in `breadcrumbHelpers` creates breadcrumb structures but never marks the final `<li>` or link as the current page with `aria‑current="page"`. Without this, screen‑reader users cannot identify which breadcrumb represents the current location.
issuesFound.fix: After locating `lastItem` in `_processNav`, add `setAttr(lastLink || lastItem, 'aria-current', 'page');`.

issuesFound.title: Site‑search combobox input lacks aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search input is given `role="combobox"` and `aria‑autocomplete="list"` but never receives an `aria‑expanded` attribute when the suggestion list is shown or hidden. This violates the ARIA combobox pattern.
issuesFound.fix: In `_open()` set `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` and in `_close()` set `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Simple billing‑toggle switch missing aria‑checked
issuesFound.explanation: The binary billing toggle (identified by `[data-billing-toggle]`) is given `role="switch"` but never receives an `aria‑checked` state, so screen readers cannot tell whether the switch is on or off.
issuesFound.fix: In `_onToggleClick()` after toggling, add `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', String(_pricingState.period === 'year'));` (and update on init).

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion triggers do not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ items use `aria‑expanded` on the trigger only during initial parsing. When a user opens or closes an item via `open(item)` or `close(item)`, the `aria‑expanded` attribute on the button is never updated, breaking the accordion ARIA pattern.
issuesFound.fix: In `open(item)` add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` and in `close(item)` add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ panels lack aria‑hidden state updates
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ panels are hidden with the `hidden` attribute, but the ARIA `aria‑hidden` property is never toggled when panels are shown or hidden. Assistive technologies may still expose hidden content.
issuesFound.fix: In `open(item)` add `setAttr(item.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'false');` and in `close(item)` add `setAttr(item.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true');`. Also ensure the panel has `role="region"` (if not already set).

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle missing aria-expanded updates

issuesFound.fix:

  Line (dropdownMenu._closeInstance): insert `setExpanded(inst.trigger, false);` after `if (!inst.open) return;`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The search combobox shows and hides its suggestion listbox without updating aria-expanded on the input. Screen readers cannot convey whether suggestions are available.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (siteSearch._open): insert `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `if (_searchState.open) return;`
  Line (siteSearch._close): insert `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `if (!_searchState.open) return;`

issuesFound.title: Form validation missing aria-invalid updates
issuesFound.explanation: When fields become invalid, aria-invalid is not set to true; when corrected, it is not cleared. Screen readers cannot convey the validation state of form fields.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav section toggle missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The button that expands and collapses mobile navigation sections does not communicate its expanded state to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 722: Add `aria-expanded={isExpanded}` to the button element: `<button type="button" aria-controls={sectionId} aria-expanded={isExpanded} className="mobile-nav__link mobile-nav__link--trigger" onClick={() => setOpenSectionId(isExpanded ? null : item.href)}>`

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The accordion trigger button lacks aria-expanded, preventing screen reader users from knowing if the answer panel is currently open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1014: Add `aria-expanded={isOpen}` to the button element: `<button type="button" id={btnId} aria-controls={panelId} aria-expanded={isOpen} className="faq-item__trigger" onClick={() => onToggle(item.id)}>`

issuesFound.title: Mobile menu toggle missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger menu button controls the mobile navigation panel but does not indicate its open/closed state programmatically.
issuesFound.fix: Line 808: Add `aria-expanded={isOpen}` to the button element: `<button id={toggleId} ref={menuToggleRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-expanded={isOpen} aria-label={isOpen ? 'Close navigation menu' : 'Open navigation menu'} className="mobile-nav-toggle" onClick={isOpen ? close : open}>`

issuesFound.title: Billing toggle buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The monthly and annual toggle buttons act as a two-state switch but do not communicate which option is selected using aria-pressed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1073: Add `aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'}` to the first button. Line 1080: Add `aria-pressed={value === 'annual'}` to the second button.

issuesFound.title: Filter tabs missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The category filter buttons toggle an active state but do not communicate selection to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 956: Add `aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value}` to the button element: `<button type="button" className={['filter-tab-btn', activeFilter === tab.value ? 'filter-tab-btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} tabIndex={activeFilter === tab.value ? 0 : -1} aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value} onClick={() => onChange(tab.value)}>`

issuesFound.title: Theme toggle button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton component requires an aria-label prop, but ThemeToggle renders it without one, leaving the icon-only button without an accessible name.
issuesFound.fix: Line 770: Add `aria-label={LABELS[theme]}` to the IconButton: `<IconButton onClick={() => setTheme(NEXT[theme])} aria-label={LABELS[theme]} className="theme-toggle">`

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel previous button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The previous slide button contains only the ‹ character and lacks an aria-label, so screen reader users cannot identify its purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1172: Add `aria-label="Previous testimonial"` to the IconButton: `<IconButton aria-controls={trackId} aria-label="Previous testimonial" onClick={handlePrev} className="testimonials-track__prev">`

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel next button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The next slide button contains only the › character and lacks an aria-label.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1177: Add `aria-label="Next testimonial"` to the IconButton: `<IconButton aria-controls={trackId} aria-label="Next testimonial" onClick={handleNext} className="testimonials-track__next">`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation close button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The close button for the mobile navigation panel is icon-only (✕) and lacks an aria-label.
issuesFound.fix: Line 698: Add `aria-label="Close navigation menu"` to the IconButton: `<IconButton onClick={onClose} aria-label="Close navigation menu" className="mobile-nav__close">`

issuesFound.title: Navigation dropdown chevron not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The ▼/▲ arrow symbol inside the dropdown trigger is announced as text by screen readers, which is redundant with the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 662: Add `aria-hidden="true"` to the span: `{isOpen ? '▲' : '▼'}`

issuesFound.title: StarRating visual symbols not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The ★ and ☆ symbols are announced as text by screen readers despite the parent element already providing an aria-label that conveys the rating.
issuesFound.fix: Line 336: Add `aria-hidden="true"` to the inner span: `{filled ? '★' : '☆'}`

issuesFound.title: Button loading spinner not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The decorative loading spinner (btn-spinner) is exposed to assistive technology while the loading state is already conveyed via the button's accessible name.
issuesFound.fix: Line 418: Add `aria-hidden="true"` to the spinner span: `<span className="btn-spinner" aria-hidden="true" />`

issuesFound.title: TextInput component missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The input element does not programmatically communicate its invalid state to assistive technology when the invalid prop is true.
issuesFound.fix: Line 500: Add `aria-invalid={invalid || undefined}` to the input: `<input id={id} aria-invalid={invalid || undefined} className={['form-input', invalid ? 'form-input--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} />`

issuesFound.title: Textarea component missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The textarea element does not communicate its invalid state programmatically.
issuesFound.fix: Line 515: Add `aria-invalid={invalid || undefined}` to the textarea: `<textarea id={id} aria-invalid={invalid || undefined} className={['form-textarea', invalid ? 'form-textarea--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} />`

issuesFound.title: Select component missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The select element does not communicate its invalid state programmatically.
issuesFound.fix: Line 537: Add `aria-invalid={invalid || undefined}` to the select: `<select id={id} aria-invalid={invalid || undefined} className={['form-select', invalid ? 'form-select--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest}>`

issuesFound.title: Pricing plan card missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The article element representing a pricing plan lacks an accessible name to distinguish it from other plans in the grid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1100: Add `aria-labelledby={headingId}` to the article: `<article aria-labelledby={headingId} className="pricing-card__article">`

issuesFound.title: Section component not applying aria-labelledby
issuesFound.explanation: The Section component accepts a labelledby prop but does not apply it to the rendered element, preventing sections from being labelled by their headings.
issuesFound.fix: Line 565: Add `aria-labelledby={labelledby}` to the Tag: `<Tag id={id} aria-labelledby={labelledby} className={['page-section', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}>`

issuesFound.title: Statistics section missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The section element lacks an aria-labelledby attribute to associate it with its visible heading (stats-heading).
issuesFound.fix: Line 874: Add `aria-labelledby="stats-heading"` to the section: `<section aria-labelledby="stats-heading" className="stats-bar">`

issuesFound.title: Hero section missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The section element lacks an aria-labelledby attribute to associate it with its heading (hero-heading).
issuesFound.fix: Line 824: Add `aria-labelledby="hero-heading"` to the section: `<section aria-labelledby="hero-heading" className="hero-section">`

issuesFound.title: Required field marker announces asterisk to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The asterisk is announced as "star" by screen readers, which is redundant with the "(required)" text provided via VisuallyHidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 476: Add `aria-hidden="true"` to the span: ` *`

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy during loading
issuesFound.explanation: The button does not communicate its loading/busy state to assistive technology when isLoading is true.
issuesFound.fix: Line 414: Add `aria-busy={isLoading}` to the button: `<button type={type} disabled={isDisabled} aria-disabled={isDisabled || undefined} aria-busy={isLoading} className={['btn', `btn--${variant}`, `btn--${size}`, className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest}>`

issuesFound.title: Desktop navigation current link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The current page link is styled visually but not identified programmatically using aria-current="page".
issuesFound.fix: Line 684: Add `aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined}` to the anchor: `<a href={item.href} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} className={['nav-link', item.current ? 'nav-link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}>`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation current link missing aria-current

issuesFound.title: Skip links navigation missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation landmark for skip links lacks an accessible name to distinguish it from other navigation regions.

issuesFound.title: Desktop navigation landmark missing accessible name

issuesFound.fix: Line 676: Add `aria-label="Main"` to the nav: `<nav id="main-nav" aria-label="Main" className="desktop-nav">`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation body missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation element inside the mobile dialog lacks an accessible name.
issuesFound.fix: Line 705: Add `aria-label="Mobile"` to the nav: `<nav aria-label="Mobile" className="mobile-nav__body">`

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form removes focused elements from DOM on submit
issuesFound.explanation: When the form submits successfully, the entire form is removed from the DOM and replaced with a success message, causing focus to be lost and potentially disorienting screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Instead of conditional rendering, use CSS to hide the form (`className={status === 'success' ? 'visually-hidden' : ''}`) while keeping it in the DOM, or move focus to the success message by adding `tabIndex={-1}` and calling `.focus()` on it.

issuesFound.title: Contact form removes focused elements from DOM on submit
issuesFound.explanation: Successful submission removes the form from the DOM, losing focus for keyboard and screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Use CSS to hide the form rather than removing it from the DOM, or move focus to the success message paragraph by adding `tabIndex={-1}` and focusing it programmatically.
